title: The finite weighted P0 average preserves constants

source.citation: S. C. Brenner and L. R. Scott, The Mathematical Theory of Finite Element Methods, 3rd ed., Springer, 2008, Ch. 4.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For a finite set with nonzero total mass, the weighted P0 average of a constant signal is that constant.
assumptions: the input is the constant c
informal.expected_conclusion: The weighted P0 average preserves constants.

declarations.name: weightedP0Average
declarations.statement: def weightedP0Average {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (m u : ι → Real) : Real := (∑ i ∈ s, m i * u i) / (∑ i ∈ s, m i)

declarations.name: weightedP0Residual
declarations.statement: def weightedP0Residual {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (m u : ι → Real) (i : ι) : Real := u i - weightedP0Average s m u

declarations.name: weightedP0Average_const
declarations.statement: theorem weightedP0Average_const {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (m : ι → Real) (c : Real) (hmass : (∑ i ∈ s, m i) ≠ 0) : weightedP0Average s m (fun _ => c) = c

tasks.input: Translate the finite-index weighted P0 projection fact `weightedP0Average_const` into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem weightedP0Average_const {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (m : ι → Real) (c : Real) (hmass : (∑ i ∈ s, m i) ≠ 0) : weightedP0Average s m (fun _ => c) = c
Use Finset.sum_mul to factor out the constant and field_simp using nonzero total mass.
